sections.paragraph:
συγγνωμονέω (later form for συγγιγνώσκω) make allowance for, pardon w. dat. (Sext. Emp., Math. 1, 126; Athen. 4, 177d; Gnomon [BGU V] 63 [II a.d.] pass.; 4 Macc 5:13; Jos., Ant. 11, 144) pardon τινί someone (Ps.-Callisth. 1, 40, 5 συγγνωμονέω ὡς θεὸς ἀνθρώποις) ITr 5:1.—DELG s.v. γιγνώσκω.

dat. dat. = dative

Sext. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Math Math = mathematician

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Gnomon Gnomon , periodical—List 6

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ps.-Callisth Ps.-Callisth , Historia Alexandri Magni, II–III? a.d.—Lists 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 951
